data.title: Bread Financial beats Q3 topline estimates
data.description:
• Bread Financial Holdings press release (NYSE:BFH): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.83 misses by $0.12.

data.title: S&P Global Non-GAAP EPS of $3.89 beats by $0.25, revenue of $3.58B beats by $150M
data.description:
• S&P Global press release (NYSE:SPGI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.89 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $3.58B (+16.2% Y/Y) beats by $150M.
  • The Company is raising full-year 2024 guidance. Guidance now calls for revenue growth of 11.5% - 12.5% vs 10.33% consensus, GAAP diluted EPS in the range of $11.95 - $12.15, and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $15.10 - $15.30 vs. $14.84 consensus

data.title: Simply Good Foods Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 in-line, revenue of $375.7M beats by $2.4M
data.description:
• Simply Good Foods press release (NASDAQ:SMPL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 in-line.
  • Revenue of $375.7M (+17.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.4M.

  • Full Fiscal Year 2025 Outlook:

    • Net sales expected to increase 8.5% to 10.5%
    • OWYN full fiscal year 2025 Net Sales expected to be in the $135-145 million range
    • Adjusted EBITDA expected to increase 4% to 6%

data.title: Toronto-Dominion Bank ratings, assessments cut by Moody's after
data.description:
Moody's Ratings downgraded the long-term ratings of Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE:TD), including its baseline credit assessment to a2 from a1, its long-term counterparty risk ratings to Aa2 from Aa1, the bank's long-term counterparty risk assessment to Aa3(cr) from Aa2(cr).

Along with those ratings actions, it cut the long-term issuer rating of TD Group US Holdings LLC to A3 from A2. In addition, TD's ratings outlooks were changed to negative from stable.

The changes came after TD Bank (TD) agreed to pay $3B to resolve investigations into its anti-money laundering practices earlier this month. Under the probes, U.S. authorities alleged that Chinese crime syndicates laundered money from illegal drug sales through the bank.

"The downgrade of TD's BCA reflects the scale and severity of the bank's risk management failures as evidenced by its BSA/AML settlement with the Department of Justice and regulators, which has changed our view of the effectiveness of TD's governance," said Robert Colangelo, Moody's Ratings vice president - senior credit officer.

data.title: Beyond Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.96 misses by $0.17, revenue of $311.43M misses by $41.36M
data.description:
• Beyond press release (NYSE:BYON): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.96 misses by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $311.43M (-16.6% Y/Y) misses by $41.36M.
  • Orders delivered of 1.6 million, a decrease of 19% year-over-year
  • Active customers of 6.0 million, an increase of 21% year-over-year

data.title: AirSculpt revises annual revenue outlook
data.description:
AirSculpt Technologies (NASDAQ:AIRS) expects a 9% Y/Y decline in its third quarter revenue, with same store cases down 8.1% Y/Y.

Q3 revenue is expected to be approximately $42.5M, compared to analysts’ estimates of $41.64M.

“While we continue to see top-line pressures primarily from macro-economic challenges, we were pleased to see that our same store cases were much improved compared to the 14% decline we experienced in the second quarter,” said Dennis Dean, interim chief executive officer and chief financial officer of AirSculpt Technologies.

Based on the preliminary revenue results for Q3, AirSculpt (AIRS) revised its full-year revenue forecast to $183M-$189M as compared to its previous guidance of $180M-190M. The company is also maintaining its full year adjusted EBITDA outlook of $23M-$28M.

AirSculpt (AIRS) will announce its Q3 results on November 8, 2024.

data.title: Rogers beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates, reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Rogers press release (NYSE:RCI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.45.
  • Revenue of $5.13B (+0.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.38B.

  • Company reaffirms 2024 outlook: Total service revenue growth of 8% to 10%; adjusted EBITDA growth of 12% to 15%; capital expenditures of $3.8 billion to $4.0 billion; and free cash flow of $2.9 billion to $3.1 billion

data.description:
• Fidelity D&D Bancorp (NASDAQ:FDBC) declared $0.40/share quarterly dividend, 5.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.38.
